result.chapter_summary: The appendix traces the historical and philosophical origins of developmentalism from Romantic religious thought through Hegel and Froebel to Elizabeth Peabody's introduction of the kindergarten in America. Hirsch argues that modern educational 'science' is actually a secularized version of the Romantic 'religion of nature' that contradicts actual neuroscientific findings regarding the plasticity of the human neocortex.

claims.text: The reigning child-centered education models, including constructivism and project-based learning, are based on a non-scientific 'religion of nature' rather than scientific foundations.
claims.source_passage: I have argued in this book that below our reigning child-centered education with its so-called “scientific” foundations of “constructivism,” and “differentiation of students,” and “project-based learning” lies the non-scientific religion of nature.

claims.text: Faith in the inherent goodness of nature underlies the misplaced confidence education professors have in their theories, despite contrary evidence.
claims.source_passage: Its faith that “nature never did betray the heart that loved her” (Wordsworth) underlies the confidence – against the evidence – that our professors of education have in the nature-sanctified soundness and rightness of their theories.

claims.text: The romantic faith in nature has resulted in the American people becoming less competent and less devoted to their country than in previous years.
claims.source_passage: I have argued in the book that this faith in nature has made the American people less competent and less devoted to America than in former years – despite our current racial progress.

claims.text: Educational equality and competence can only be achieved by repudiating the romantic scheme of education.
claims.source_passage: I have also argued that the road to equality and competence lies in repudiating the whole romantic scheme of education that has gradually transfixed our minds over the decades.

claims.text: The successful spread of Romantic religious ideas into the secular world was achieved through a process of secularization.
claims.source_passage: The secularization of the religion of nature was the hallmark of the successful spread of religious ideas.

claims.text: Modern educational research literature uses a mien of scientific rigor to allow romanticism to influence secular minds.
claims.source_passage: The secularization of the romantic religion of nature in our current educational research literature, with its mien of hardheadedness and scientific rigor is the technique that enabled romanticism to take over some of the ablest secular minds of the western world.

claims.text: The English translation of Hegel's 'Geist' as 'Mind' was a secular 'cover-up' for what was originally intended as 'Holy Spirit.'
claims.source_passage: Hegel’s seminal work Phänomenologie des Geistes – The Phenomenology of Spirit was translated into English under the title The Phenomenology of Mind. That was a secular cover-up. term for the Holy Spirit is heiliger Geist.

claims.text: Hegel's phenomenology describes the unfolding of the Divine (Geist) within historical human history.
claims.source_passage: So if the noumenon is Geist (God), what Hegel describes as the unfolding of thought in the world is nothing less than the manifest unfolding of the Divine within the historical human world.

claims.text: Friedrich Froebel, the inventor of the kindergarten, was significantly influenced by the philosopher Friedrich Schelling.
claims.source_passage: Froebel acknowledged that he was influenced by Schelling.

claims.text: The pedagogical traditions of the kindergarten introduced by Elizabeth Peabody eventually expanded to dominate later school grades in 20th-century America.
claims.source_passage: Her tradition, as we have seen, was also to take over the later grades in America in the 20th century.

claims.text: Froebel’s educational philosophy incorrectly suggests that humans are born with an instinctive blueprint similar to animals like ducks or chickens.
claims.source_passage: Notice too how Froebel uses the animal world to suggest that humans, like ducks and chickens, are born with an instinctive blueprint.

claims.text: Contrary to Froebel's view, the human neocortex is highly moldable and characterized by 'cortical plasticity.'
claims.source_passage: We know that being moldable like clay is precisely the case with that part of the brain that we try to educate in kindergarten and the later grades – the neocortex. Moldability is the very metaphor now in use in frontline neuroscientific research on the human brain.

claims.text: Modern educational concepts like 'discovery learning' and 'guide-on-the-side-ism' are direct descendants of Froebel’s romantic faith in nature.
claims.source_passage: That excerpt will illustrate how faith in nature morphed into “constructivism,” “project-based instruction,” “discovery learning,” “differentiation,” and “guide on-the-side-ism,”

claims.text: Froebel's system of education is founded upon a deeper insight into the nature of children than any other, with the exception of Jesus.
claims.source_passage: This system embodies the wisdom of ages and is founded upon a deeper insight into the nature of children than has been expressed by any others, with the exception of him who pronounced them “of the kingdom of Heaven.”

claims.text: Children who do not receive proper early education often have much to unlearn by the age of ten.
claims.source_passage: He had been for ten years engaged with friends in an attempt to educate children, who come to him at ten years old, and who, he found, had at that age much to unlearn.

claims.text: During the first seven years of life, mothers are the only persons competent to educate children into a harmony of heart, intellect, and hand.
claims.source_passage: His work is addressed to mothers, whom he thought at the moment the only persons competent to educate children into the harmony of heart, intellect, and hand, during the first seven years of their age.

claims.text: The kindergarten was invented because individual mothers lack the strength to provide all the nurture due to children's natures.
claims.source_passage: But in the course of the next twelve years he learned that no mortal mother could have the strength to do all that is due to children in order that justice may be done to their natures, but that she must have assistance ; and he invented the kindergarten in 1839

claims.text: Reading instruction should not begin until age seven, after a child's perceptive and artistic faculties have been cultivated.
claims.source_passage: be played with in the mother’s genial, cherishing way till old enough to be sent to school and taught to read at seven years of age, which he thought early enough to teach them the signs of the ideas they would have acquired by the cultivation of their perceptive and artistic faculties

claims.text: Instruction must be passive and following rather than dictatorial and prescriptive to avoid destroying the child's nature.
claims.source_passage: Therefore, from this point of view, education and all instruction must be much more passive and following than dictatorial and prescriptive

claims.text: A young human being instinctively wills that which is best for himself in a form suitable to his own power.
claims.source_passage: This necessity must presuppose that the still young human being, even though as yet unconsciously, like a product of Nature, precisely and surely wills that which is best for himself

claims.text: External interference disturbs the pure unfolding and healthy development of young plants, animals, and humans.
claims.source_passage: we let them rest, and strive to avoid powerfully interfering influences upon them, knowing that these influences disturb their pure unfolding and healthy development

claims.text: Treating a child like a 'piece of wax' or 'lump of clay' to be molded is a fundamental error in education.
claims.source_passage: but the young human being is to man a piece of wax, a lump of clay, from which he can mold what he will.

claims.text: Constraints and lack of free space hide the inner symmetry and life of a developing being.
claims.source_passage: Look at the plants which you call weeds, and which, grown up here compressed and constrained, scarcely permit one to guess at their inner symmetry but look at them in free space, in field and flowerbed, and see what a symmetry, what a pure inner life they show

claims.text: Imposing a vocation or form on a child against their nature causes them to live in languor and unnaturalness.
claims.source_passage: So, parents, your children on whom you early impress form and vocation against their nature, and who therefore wander around you in languor and unnaturalness, might also become beautiful, self-unfolding, and all-sided self-developing beings.

claims.text: All active, dictatorial, and forcibly interfering instruction destroys the 'divine' element in a child's healthy state.
claims.source_passage: All active, dictatorial, invariable, and forcibly interfering education and instruction must necessarily have a disturbing, checking, and destructive effect upon the action of the divine

claims.text: Educational 'pruning' is harmful if it does not passively follow the nature of the child.
claims.source_passage: the pruning, as such, brings no more wine from the grape vine. Rather the grape vine may be wholly destroyed by the pruning, however good may be the intention in doing it; at least, its fruitfulness and capacity for bearing fruit are injured if the gardener, in his work, does not passively and thoughtfully follow the nature of the plant.

claims.text: The laws governing the management of nature and the management of human beings are the same.
claims.source_passage: And yet there act in both powers which flow from one fountain, and which act according to the same laws.

claims.text: Human nature should be presupposed to be in an unviolated, original condition until proven otherwise.
claims.source_passage: but so much the more must it be presupposed, especially of the human being, so long as the opposite has not expressed itself

claims.text: All education is destroyed when it relies on external pressure, law, or hate, which produce slavery, deceit, and contumacy.
claims.source_passage: All education every effect of education, — teaching, and instruction is destroyed where hate produces hate, where law produces deceit and crime, where pressure produces slavery and necessity servitude

claims.text: Arbitrariness in education is only banished when the teacher is himself subjected to a perpetually governing law or necessity.
claims.source_passage: This takes place when all education... has this undeniable, resistless imprint, that the requiring one himself is strictly and inevitably subjected to a perpetually governing law

claims.text: All human language is inherently ambiguous.
claims.source_passage: All human language is inherently ambiguous.

claims.text: Language is disambiguated by applying unspoken, unwritten background knowledge shared between the sender and receiver.
claims.source_passage: Language is disambiguated by applying unspoken, unwritten background knowledge that is silently shared between sender and receiver.

claims.text: There is no significant 'complexity-managing' reading skill that operates independently of relevant background knowledge.
claims.source_passage: There exists a significant complexity-managing reading skill, independent of relevant knowledge. [Answer: F]

claims.text: Human tribes thrive through concerted action enabled by educating youth in shared language conventions.
claims.source_passage: Evolutionary psychology tells us that human tribes thrive through concerted action enabled by educating their young in the language conventions

claims.text: The modern industrial nation tends to be monolingual.
claims.source_passage: The modern industrial nation tends to be monolingual.

claims.text: Successful multilingual nations like Switzerland rely on a common school culture that provides shared background knowledge across linguistic divides.
claims.source_passage: Successful multilingual ones (as in Switzerland) are largely monocultural in a common school culture, so that all citizens share a lot of the same background knowledge.

claims.text: The common language of a modern society is a standardized print language characterized by standard spelling, grammar, and pronunciation taught in schools.
claims.source_passage: The common language of a modern society is a standardized print language with standard spelling, grammar, and pronunciation taught in the nation’s schools.

claims.text: Effective use of a nation's print language depends on schools imparting a standardized print-culture of shared background knowledge to students.
claims.source_passage: Effective use of the print language in the nation depends on its schools imparting the print-culture – standardized shared background knowledge -- to its young, thus enabling effective communication.

claims.text: A reading test is effectively a test of shared background knowledge.
claims.source_passage: A reading test is in effect a shared-knowledge test.

claims.text: There does not exist a significant, transferable 'main-idea-finding' skill independent of specific knowledge.
claims.source_passage: There exists a significant main-idea-finding skill. [Answer: F]

claims.text: A young teenager's score on a reading test is a predictor of their level of income in later life.
claims.source_passage: On average, a young teen’s score on a reading test predicts that person’s level of income in later life.

claims.text: A majority of American elementary schools reduced the teaching of shared background knowledge around 1940 under the banner of child-centered education.
claims.source_passage: Around 1940, under the banner of “child-centered education,” a majority of American elementary schools began teaching less of the commonly shared background knowledge of the print culture.

claims.text: A reading test functions, in effect, as a shared-knowledge test.
claims.source_passage: A reading test is in effect a shared-knowledge test.

claims.text: American reading test scores have declined since 1950, even among college-bound students.
claims.source_passage: Since 1950 scores on reading tests have declined – even among our most ambitious, college-bound students.

claims.text: Significant differences in the learning styles of different students do not exist.
claims.source_passage: There exists a significant difference in the learning styles of different students. [Answer: F]

claims.text: The French school system's detailed records allow for the observation of educational reforms with the decisiveness of a laboratory experiment.
claims.source_passage: The French keep unusually detailed school achievement records, so when they adopted child-centered schooling the detailed results are as decisive as a lab experiment with millions of subjects.

claims.text: The French adoption of child-centered schooling and the abandonment of common grade-by-grade topics led to a decline in verbal scores.
claims.source_passage: This is a true graph of what happened to French education, when it adopted the American, child-centered theory of schooling, abandoning their teaching of common grade-by-grade topics to all students.

claims.text: Social justice in France, as measured by educational equity, declined following the implementation of child-centered schooling.
claims.source_passage: And this is a true graph of what happened to French social justice in the same period.

claims.text: A reading test is effectively an 'ethnicity test' in disguise, measuring initiation into the national print culture.
claims.source_passage: A reading test is an ethnicity test in disguise.

claims.text: The SAT Verbal score in the United States declined by nearly 0.8 standard deviations between 1952 and 2012.
claims.source_passage: Percent of Standard Deviation Decline of SAT Verbal By decade 1952–2012 [Graph]

claims.text: Monocultural school cultures, such as those in Switzerland, ensure that all citizens share significant background knowledge.
claims.source_passage: Switzerland) are largely monocultural in a common school culture, so that all citizens share a lot of the same background knowledge.

claims.text: French reading scores across all socio-economic groups declined significantly between 1987 and 2007.
claims.source_passage: French Reading Scores 1987–2007

claims.text: Even the highest socio-economic group in France—Executive and Intellectual Professionals—experienced a substantial decline in reading scores between 1987 and 2007.
claims.source_passage: French Reading Scores 1987–2007 [Chart data showing Executive & Intellectual Professionals declining from roughly 0.8 to 0.4 standard deviations]

claims.text: Educational data and conclusions based on that data can cause offense and annoyance to highly placed people in the education field.
claims.source_passage: John Ballen... has encouraged me to go ahead and state what the data say, even if some feelings get hurt, and some highly placed people get annoyed.

claims.text: American education schools are prone to making significant scientific errors.
claims.source_passage: Daniel Willingham has been... a dear friend who has saved me from excessive absolutism in my disparagement of the scientific errors of our education schools.

claims.text: The Core Knowledge Foundation successfully distributed tens of thousands of free educational materials to parents and teachers during the Covid pandemic.
claims.source_passage: during the Covid pandemic it has been able to help parents and teachers make download tens of thousands of excellent and free school materials.

claims.text: The work of Jeffrey Karpicke is central to understanding the effective mechanisms of learning and memory.
claims.source_passage: Pino Trogu, who introduced me to the key work of Jeffrey Karpicke

claims.text: The philosopher Hegel exercised a strong and permanent influence on John Dewey, who admitted that Hegelian thought left a 'permanent deposit' in his mind.
claims.source_passage: The prize example has been Hegel – who exercised a strong influence upon John Dewey. (Dewey said that Hegel had left a “permanent deposit” in his mind.)

claims.text: While the American people have become less competent and devoted due to educational shifts, the country has simultaneously made significant racial progress.
claims.source_passage: I have argued in the book that this faith in nature has made the American people less competent and less devoted to America than in former years – despite our current racial progress.

claims.text: The translation of 'Geist' as 'Mind' in Hegel's work served as a secular cover-up for the term's original religious meaning of 'Holy Spirit' (heiliger Geist).
claims.source_passage: Hegel’s seminal work Phänomenologie des Geistes... was translated into English under the title The Phenomenology of Mind. That was a secular cover-up. term for the Holy Spirit is heiliger Geist.

claims.text: Frontline neuroscientific research on 'cortical plasticity' focuses specifically on the 'microcircuits' of the neocortex.
claims.source_passage: A whole branch of the subject is busily concerning itself with “cortical plasticity” at the most fundamental “microcircuits” of the neocortex.

claims.text: Prescriptive or 'strictly requiring' methods of education are only justified when it is certain that a child's original, unviolated condition has been infracted.
claims.source_passage: But if the certainty of the infraction of the original proceeds from the totality of the human being who is to be educated... in that case, strictly requiring ways of education enter in their full force.

claims.text: Directing education only becomes appropriate after the start of 'connected life' and understanding between the elder (father/master) and the youth.
claims.source_passage: Therefore the purely requiring, defining, and directing way of education begins first with the educating of himself... after the beginning of understanding and the common life between father and son, youth and master

claims.text: True instruction requires that external law must call forth internal self-determination, and external pressure must call forth free will.
claims.source_passage: In good education, in genuine instruction, in true teaching, therefore, necessity must call forth freedom; and law, self-determination the pressure from without, the free will within

claims.text: The only way to banish 'arbitrariness' in education is for the teacher to be visibly subject to a perpetual governing law and necessity themselves.
claims.source_passage: This takes place when all education... has this undeniable, resistless imprint, that the requiring one himself is strictly and inevitably subjected to a perpetually governing law, to an unavoidable perpetual necessity; thus all arbitrariness is banished.

evidence.description: John Dewey's own admission of the 'permanent deposit' Hegel's philosophy left in his mind.
The linguistic transition of 'Geist' to 'Mind' in the English translation of Hegel's work.
The historical fact that Froebel attended the University of Jena while Schelling and Hegel were lecturing there.
Frontline neuroscientific research on 'cortical plasticity' at the level of microcircuits in the neocortex.
Elizabeth Peabody’s 1885 American Preface to Froebel’s 'The Education of Man.'

The instinctive behaviors of ducklings (seeking water), chickens (scratching), and swallows (catching food on the wing) are used to prove that young beings know what is best for their own development.
The observation that weeds grown in compressed spaces look asymmetrical, while those in free spaces show perfect inner symmetry and beauty.
The analogy of the grape vine, where pruning alone does not produce wine and can destroy fruitfulness if the gardener ignores the plant's nature.
The survival and concerted action of human tribes as explained by evolutionary principles.
Switzerland as an example of a successful multilingual nation unified by a common school culture.
Statistical correlation between teen reading scores and adult income levels.
The shift in American elementary school curriculum beginning around 1940.
Graph showing the decline of SAT Verbal scores by decade from 1952 to 2012, showing a drop of approximately 0.8 standard deviations.
Graph correlating Armed Forces Qualifying Test (AFQT) scores at age 15–19 with logged household income/poverty status at age 27–31, showing a positive linear relationship.

Graph of French reading scores from 1987 to 2007 broken down by professional class, showing that scores for children of laborers and the unemployed dropped significantly more (nearly 1.2 standard deviations) than scores for children of executive/intellectual professionals.

A statistical chart showing a consistent downward trend in reading scores for five social categories in France (Executive/Intellectual, Unemployed, Laborers, Artisans/Shop Owners/Farmers/White Collar, and Intermediate Professionals) over two decades.
Download records of the Core Knowledge Foundation during the Covid pandemic.
evidence.source_reference: Core Knowledge Foundation internal data (cited in acknowledgements)

concepts.author_definition: A non-scientific, Romantic faith that nature is inherently right and should guide human development/education, often masked by secular or scientific language.
The technique of re-presenting religious or metaphysical concepts in secular, often 'scientific' or philosophical terms to gain acceptance in post-Enlightenment culture.
The fundamental moldability of the neocortex's microcircuits, which allows for education and social molding, contradicting the idea of a fixed natural blueprint.
The German term for 'Spirit' (specifically the Holy Spirit in Hegel's original theological context), later translated as 'Mind' to sound more secular.
An approach to instruction that watchfully and protectively follows the child's natural instincts rather than directing or imposing external forms.
A setting where children from multiple families are gathered under a 'mother’s assistant' to play in a genial, cherishing way before starting formal schooling at age seven.
The undisturbed, healthy development of a human being in conformity with the internal laws of their nature.

concepts.term: Harmony of heart, intellect, and hand
concepts.author_definition: The integrated goal of early childhood development, prioritizing holistic growth over narrow academic skills.
The standardized shared background knowledge required to effectively use a nation's common print language.
Proposed transferable abilities like 'comprehension' or 'critical thinking' that the author argues do not exist independently of specific knowledge.
concepts.common_alternative: The mainstream educational belief that reading and thinking are transferable skills that can be practiced on any content.

concepts.author_definition: The idea that reading tests do not measure a generic skill but rather a student's degree of initiation into the 'national ethnicity' or shared communicative store of the society.
A theory of schooling, often called 'developmentalism,' that abandoned the grade-by-grade teaching of common topics in favor of following student interests.
The systematic failure of teacher-training institutions to align their pedagogical theories with the established findings of cognitive science.
The unknowable 'thing in itself' as defined by Kant, which Hegel identifies as the 'Being of God.'
A colloquial term for the passive role of the teacher in developmentalist education, which the author claims is at odds with science.
The fundamental level of the neocortex where cortical plasticity and educational molding occur.
The presupposed healthy state of a child which Froebel argues education should passively follow and protect from interference.
A language with standard spelling, grammar, and pronunciation taught in schools that serves as the common medium of a modern industrial nation.

cases.name: Introduction of English-speaking Kindergarten (1860)
cases.description: Elizabeth Peabody introduced the kindergarten system to America in 1860, based on Froebel's developmentalist theories. This served as the entry point for Romantic pedagogical ideas into the American school system, eventually spreading into higher grades.
key_passages: Elizabeth Peabody (who introduced our English-speaking kindergarten in 1860)
Her tradition, as we have seen, was also to take over the later grades in America in the 20th century.

cases.name: Translation of Hegel's Phenomenology of Spirit
cases.description: The translation of Hegel's 'Phänomenologie des Geistes' into English as 'The Phenomenology of Mind' is used as a specific case of secularizing religious/metaphysical content to fit into secular high culture.
key_passages: Hegel’s seminal work Phänomenologie des Geistes – The Phenomenology of Spirit was translated into English under the title The Phenomenology of Mind. That was a secular cover-up.

cases.name: Froebel's invention of the Kindergarten (1839)
cases.description: After ten years of educating ten-year-olds and finding they had much to unlearn, Froebel realized mothers needed professional assistance. He created the kindergarten as a transitional space for 12-25 children to play for three hours daily before beginning formal school at age seven.
key_passages: he invented the kindergarten in 1839, in which he proposed that from twelve to twenty-five children should be gathered for three hours every day, from several families, under the care of a mother’s assistant, whom he called a kindergartner

cases.name: Switzerland
cases.description: A multilingual nation that maintains national unity and effectiveness through a common, monocultural school culture that provides shared background knowledge.
key_passages: Successful multilingual ones (as in Switzerland) are largely monocultural in a common school culture, so that all citizens share a lot of the same background knowledge.

cases.name: American Schools 1940-Present
cases.description: The historical period where American elementary education adopted 'child-centered' ideologies, leading to a decline in the teaching of common background knowledge.
key_passages: Around 1940, under the banner of “child-centered education,” a majority of American elementary schools began teaching less of the commonly shared background knowledge of the print culture.

cases.name: France 1987-2007
cases.description: The author characterizes France's educational shift during this period as a 'lab experiment' in child-centered schooling. By adopting American-style child-centered theories and abandoning a common grade-by-grade curriculum, France experienced a massive decline in average verbal scores and a significant widening of the achievement gap between social classes.
key_passages: The French keep unusually detailed school achievement records, so when they adopted child-centered schooling the detailed results are as decisive as a lab experiment with millions of subjects.
This is a true graph of what happened to French education, when it adopted the American, child-centered theory of schooling, abandoning their teaching of common grade-by-grade topics to all students.
And this is a true graph of what happened to French social justice in the same period.
contestable_aspects: Causal link between child-centered theory and the specific score decline
Characterization of the reforms as purely 'American' or 'child-centered'
The use of 'social justice' to describe standard deviation gaps between professions

cases.name: United States post-1940
cases.description: Beginning around 1940, American elementary schools moved toward child-centered education, leading to a decrease in the teaching of shared print-culture knowledge. This shift is linked by the author to a long-term decline in SAT Verbal scores and other reading metrics.
key_passages: Around 1940, under the banner of “child-centered education,” a majority of American elementary schools began teaching less of the commonly shared background knowledge of the print culture.
Since 1950 scores on reading tests have declined – even among our most ambitious, college-bound students.

thinkers.name: Friedrich Froebel
thinkers.key_idea: The child possesses an instinctive blueprint like an animal and should not be molded like clay; education should facilitate this natural development.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch argues Froebel's mockery of the 'clay' metaphor is scientifically incorrect regarding the neocortex and that his 'nature' focus morphed into modern failing educational practices.
key_passages: Froebel mocks the idea that the human child is moldable like a piece of clay.
Notice too how Froebel uses the animal world to suggest that humans, like ducks and chickens, are born with an instinctive blueprint.

thinkers.name: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel
thinkers.key_idea: The unfolding of 'Geist' (Spirit) in history as the manifest unfolding of the Divine.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Hegel to illustrate how developmentalist ideas were originally deeply religious/metaphysical before being secularized for wider consumption.
key_passages: what Hegel describes as the unfolding of thought in the world is nothing less than the manifest unfolding of the Divine within the historical human world.

thinkers.name: Elizabeth Peabody
thinkers.key_idea: Froebel's system embodies the 'wisdom of ages' and provides the only competent education for the first seven years of life.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch views her as the primary conduit for bringing these 'romantic' and ultimately harmful developmentalist ideas to America.
key_passages: This system embodies the wisdom of ages and is founded upon a deeper insight into the nature of children than has been expressed by any others

thinkers.name: Miss Blow of St. Louis
thinkers.key_idea: An eminent teacher who used Froebel's 'Mother-Love and Nursery Songs' manual for training kindergartners.
thinkers.engagement: Cited as an example of how Froebelian methods were institutionalized in American teacher training.
key_passages: This book has been used as the Manual for training Kindergartners by Miss Blow of St. Louis, and other eminent teachers.

thinkers.name: Daniel Willingham
thinkers.key_idea: The application of cognitive science to classroom practice and the identification of scientific flaws in prevailing educational theories.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch credits Willingham with providing the scientific grounding for his critique and for tempering his 'absolutism' regarding the failures of education schools.
key_passages: The distinguished cognitive scientist, Daniel Willingham has been a long-term scientific resource, and a dear friend who has saved me from excessive absolutism in my disparagement of the scientific errors of our education schools.

thinkers.name: Jeffrey Karpicke
thinkers.key_idea: Retrieval practice and the 'testing effect' as a more effective way to learn than repetitive studying.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch identifies Karpicke's work as 'key' to the book's argument, likely regarding how children best acquire and retain background knowledge.
key_passages: Pino Trogu, who introduced me to the key work of Jeffrey Karpicke

actors.name: John Dewey
actors.context: Cited as being heavily influenced by Hegel, carrying the 'permanent deposit' of Hegelian thought into American education.

actors.name: Friedrich Schelling
actors.context: Romantic philosopher and roommate of Hegel; identified as a major influence on Froebel.

actors.name: Professors of Education
actors.context: Accused of having misplaced confidence in non-scientific theories based on the 'religion of nature.'

actors.name: Josephine Jarvis
actors.context: Translator of Froebel's works into English.

actors.name: Mrs. Quincy Shaw
actors.context: Provided munificent assistance to publish Froebel's manuals in Boston.

actors.name: Elizabeth Peabody
actors.context: Introduced Froebel's developmentalist ideas to the American context.

actors.name: American Elementary Schools
actors.context: The site of the shift from knowledge-based to child-centered education around 1940.

actors.name: Natasha Tobin
actors.context: The author's wife, credited with providing decisive help in writing the book.

actors.name: Core Knowledge Foundation
actors.context: The organization that develops and distributes the curriculum materials Hirsch advocates for; provided free resources during the pandemic.

actors.name: John Ballen
actors.context: Chairman of the Board of the Core Knowledge Foundation who encouraged Hirsch to follow the data regardless of controversy.

actors.name: Natasha Tobin
actors.context: Hirsch's wife and editor who ensured the book's readability for a general audience.

actors.name: Linda Bevilacqua
actors.context: President of the Core Knowledge Foundation, described as the 'rock' of the effort for three decades.

objections_raised.objection: Educational theories like constructivism and differentiation are based on scientific research.
objections_raised.response: This 'research' is at odds with actual science (like neuroscience) in other parts of the university; it is merely romanticism adopting a scientific 'mien.'

objections_raised.objection: The truth of 'cautious following' (passive education) and its application to human beings is frequently contested.
objections_raised.response: These truths will vindicate themselves to the generation that applies them with full confidence.

objections_raised.objection: Reading comprehension and critical thinking are general skills that can be taught independently of content knowledge.
objections_raised.response: The quiz asserts these are false; reading tests are actually tests of specific background knowledge, and comprehension depends on that knowledge.

objections_raised.objection: Students have significantly different learning styles that schools should accommodate.
objections_raised.response: The author labels the existence of significant differences in learning styles as 'False.'

objections_raised.objection: The data presented in the book might be offensive or annoying to established educational authorities.
objections_raised.response: The author must state 'what the data say' regardless of whether 'feelings get hurt' or 'highly placed people get annoyed.'

cross_chapter_refs.reference_text: In the chapter on developmentalism I analyzed the religious under­pinnings of developmentalism.
